r11FindingsStatus.summary: --only-missing validates existing published packages before skipping and fails closed on stale sidecars.
--resume-interrupted executes staging cleanup, re-enters backfill, and writes execution-record.json.
--rollback-interrupted removes invalid live manifest/publish residue, re-enters backfill, and writes execution-record.json.
Conflict, validation, protected metadata, and fsync failure paths exit non-zero without publishing invalid packages.
Read-only validation shows 38 already_migrated published packages, 34 residues, no partial/failed interrupted states, and existing evidence with processed=0 skipped=38 failed=0.

criteriaResults.summary: Classifier distinguishes completed books, residues, partial migrations, failed interruptions, repair-required roots, and already migrated packages.
Manifest generation is gated by distribution manifest sidecars, canonical input, source closure, qmd/graph manifests, producer evidence, and artifact checksums.
Package layout and manifest locators are package-relative, with mount.packageRoot validated as '.'.
BOOK_MANIFEST, PUBLISH_READY, sidecars, embedded checksums, publish marker checksum, and file closure checksums are regenerated and validated.
Residues are isolated from publish, mount, export, and catalog projection without deleting legacy evidence.
Already migrated packages are verify-only; interrupted states require explicit recovery flags and either execute recovery or fail closed.
Duplicate source hash, same bookId different sourceHash, manifest identity, staging, and live-root conflicts have stable fail-closed diagnostics.
Migration evidence and execution-record.json provide auditable plan, checkpoint, rollback, commit, and recovery decisions.
Catalog projection is rebuilt only from validated published packages; real evidence shows completed books skipped and residues excluded.
Backfill tests cover only-missing validation, verify-only reruns, resume, rollback, protected metadata, fsync failure, and conflict fail-closed behavior.
